You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 07297749).

## Issue to Resolve

Wrap annotations before class constructor (ktlint_official)
Annotations before the class constructor should be wrapped in same way as other annotations when code style `ktlint_official` is used.

Example:
```
data class Foo @Bar1 @Bar2("bar") @Bar3 @Bar4 constructor(private val foobar: Int) {
    fun foo(): String = "foo"
}
```
should be formatted as
```
data class Foo
    @Bar1
    @Bar2("bar")
    @Bar3
    @Bar4
    constructor(private val foobar: Int) {
    fun foo(): String = "foo"
}
```
Annotations on type projections should be wrapped in same way as other annotations
Given a type argument list in which one or more of the type projects are annotation than the same wrapping rules should be applies as with other annotations.

Exampe:
```
val fooBar: FooBar<String, @Foo String, @Foo @Bar String, @Bar("bar") @Foo String> = FooBar()
```
should be formatted as:
```
val fooBar: FooBar<
    String,
    @Foo String,
    @Foo @Bar
    String,
    @Bar("bar")
    @Foo
    String
    > = FooBar()
```

In case the type argument list only contains type projections without or with a single annotation without parameter than the annotation does not need to be wrapped. So code below will not be changed:
```
val foo1: List<@Foo String> = emptyList()
val foo2 = emptyList<@Foo String>()
val foo3: Map<@Foo String, @Foo String> = emptyMap()
val foo4 = emptyMap<@Foo String, @Foo String>()
```

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-cli/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/cli/api/Baseline.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/AnnotationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/TypeArgumentListSpacingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

414â†’        node: ASTNode,
415â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
416â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
417â†’    ) {
418â†’        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
419â†’
420â†’        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
421â†’            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
422â†’        ) {
423â†’            emit(
424â†’                node.startOffset,
425â†’                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
426â†’                true,
427â†’            )
428â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
429â†’                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
430â†’                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
431â†’                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
432â†’                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
433â†’                }
434â†’            }
435â†’        }
436â†’    }

438â†’    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
439â†’        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
440â†’            modifierListRoot
441â†’                .treeParent
442â†’                .treePrev
443â†’        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
444â†’        val indentWithoutNewline =
445â†’            nodeBeforeAnnotations
446â†’                ?.text
447â†’                .orEmpty()
448â†’                .substringAfterLast('\n')
449â†’        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
450â†’    }
451â†’}

 168â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(

 198â†’
 199â†’            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||

 299â†’
 300â†’            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 301â†’                visitWhenEntry(node)

 303â†’            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&

 310â†’            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||

 336â†’    private fun visitValueArgument(node: ASTNode) {

 52â†’                // unless it is part of a type reference:
 53â†’                //    fun foo(): List<Foo> { ... }
 54â†’                //    var bar: List<Bar> = emptyList()
 55â†’                it.isPartOfCompositeElementOfType(ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE)
 56â†’            }
 57â†’            ?.takeUnless {
 58â†’                // unless it is part of a call expression followed by lambda:
 59â†’                //    bar<Foo> { ... }
 60â†’                it.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda()
 61â†’            }
 62â†’            ?.lastChildNode
 63â†’            ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 64â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 65â†’            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 66â†’    }

 68â†’    private fun visitInsideTypeArgumentList(
 69â†’        node: ASTNode,
 70â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 71â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 72â†’    ) {
 73â†’        // No whitespace expected after opening angle bracket of type argument list
 74â†’        //    val list = listOf< String>()
 75â†’        node
 76â†’            .findChildByType(ElementType.LT)
 77â†’            ?.nextSibling()
 78â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 79â†’            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 80â†’
 81â†’        // No whitespace expected before closing angle bracket of type argument list
 82â†’        //    val list = listOf<String >()
 83â†’        node
 84â†’            .findChildByType(ElementType.GT)
 85â†’            ?.prevSibling()
 86â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 87â†’            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 88â†’    }
 89â†’
 90â†’    private fun noWhitespaceExpected(
 91â†’        node: ASTNode,
 92â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 93â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94â†’    ) {
 95â†’        if (node.text != "") {
 96â†’            emit(
 97â†’                node.startOffset,
 98â†’                "No whitespace expected at this position",
 99â†’                true,
100â†’            )
101â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
102â†’                node.treeParent.removeChild(node)
103â†’            }
104â†’        }
105â†’    }
106â†’}
107â†’
108â†’private fun ASTNode.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda(): Boolean =
109â†’    findCompositeParentElementOfType(ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION)
110â†’        ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION }
111â†’        ?.findChildByType(ElementType.LAMBDA_ARGUMENT)
112â†’        .let { it != null }
113â†’

390â†’        node: ASTNode,
391â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,

394â†’        node
395â†’            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
396â†’            .nextCodeLeaf()
397â†’            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
398â†’                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
399â†’

418â†’        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)

422â†’        ) {

438â†’    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
439â†’        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
440â†’            modifierListRoot
441â†’                .treeParent
442â†’                .treePrev
443â†’        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
444â†’        val indentWithoutNewline =
445â†’            nodeBeforeAnnotations
446â†’                ?.text
447â†’                .orEmpty()
448â†’                .substringAfterLast('\n')
449â†’        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
